mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount factors, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate hikes are positive in > 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 60% of triggered instances over a 5-year backtest, or t-statistic for return difference is below 1.5.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in retail sales and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and business demand expansion, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive changes in RSAFS and HOUST yield negative forward returns in > 50% of cases over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the industrial production index during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases during easing cycles fail to predict positive 5-day returns with a t-statistic below 1.5 over a 4-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the CPIAUCSL index exceeding 0.3 points signals disinflationary pressure that reduces corporate input costs and supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL declines of this magnitude are followed by negative forward returns in > 55% of observations over a 3-year rolling period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between declining total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness and impending recession, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The PAYEMS-UNRATE divergence signal yields positive forward returns in > 50% of cases over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP reflects expanding corporate revenue bases and economic momentum, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the industrial production index exceeding 0.5 points signals a contraction in manufacturing activity and supply chain bottlenecks, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines of this magnitude are followed by positive forward returns in > 60% of triggered instances over a 4-year sample.
